docs.title: Vision
docs.text: Space sensing forms the foundation of space exploration, enabling humanity’s future.

docs.title: Mission
docs.text: To enable the continuous identification, monitoring, and resolution of critical technology gaps to full and efficient space sensing for humanity.

docs.title: Scope
docs.text:
The Akron Project focuses exclusively on civilian, scientific, commercial, environmental, and humanitarian applications of space sensing technologies.

Military, defence, intelligence, and weapon-related applications, both on Earth and in Space, are explicitly out of scope.

docs.title: Problem Statement
docs.text:
The problem is multifaceted and stems from three interconnected challenges:

  1. Humanity lacks a clear understanding of how advances in space sensing directly contribute to solving today’s global challenges.
  2. There is no unified, prioritised global framework that identifies and categorises the critical technology gaps required for full and efficient space sensing.
  3. The current approach to addressing space sensing technology gaps is not optimised at a systems level.

docs.title: Impact
docs.text:
1. Policy-makers, educators, investors, and international institutions are hindered from making fully informed decisions.
  2. Research and development efforts remain fragmented across disciplines, nations, and organisations, slowing progress and duplicating investment.
  3. International cooperation is inconsistent, educational pathways are insufficiently aligned with future technological needs, and public and private investments are prioritised without a long-term, coordinated strategy.

docs.title: Proposed Solution
docs.text: Develop and maintain a curated list of technology gaps, highlighting their impact on both space sensing and global challenges on Earth.

docs.title: Cognitive Map / Information Model
docs.text:
%%{init: {'flowchart': {'curve': 'basis'}}}%%
flowchart 

    subgraph space_exploration[Space Exploration]
        direction BT
                access[Space Access] -. depends on .-> sensing[Space Sensing]:::focus
                utilisation[Space Utilisation] -. depends on .-> access
    end

    technologies[Technologies]:::focus -. used for .-> space_apps[Space Applications]:::focus
    space_apps -. enable .-> space_exploration

    %%gap[<b>Technology Gaps,<br/>'The Akron List'<b>]:::focus -. block .-> technology
    technologies -. have .-> gaps[<b>Technology Gaps,<br/>'The Akron List'<b>]:::focus  
    gaps:::focus -. constrain .-> space_apps
    gaps:::focus -. constrain .-> earth_apps

    technologies -. used for .-> earth_apps[Earth Applications]:::focus
    earth_apps -. resolve .-> global_challenges[Global Challenges]:::focus



    classDef focus fill:#90EE90,color:#000,stroke:#333,stroke-width:2px

Green boxes show the Akron Project's focus.

docs.title: Technology Gaps, “The Akron List”
docs.text: A structured catalogue of Technology Gaps that simultaneously constrain Space Sensing and Earth Applications. Each record captures the current state, required capability advances, mapped technologies, applications, SDGs, relevant R&D fields, and linked evidence, enabling systematic gap analysis, prioritisation, and research planning.

docs.title: AKRON-1: Miniaturised High-Sensitivity Sensors
docs.text:
• Taxonomy:
  • Sensors: Cross-cutting sensor subsystems
  • Gap summary:
  • High-sensitivity sensors remain too large or power-hungry for small platforms, limiting small probes and dense terrestrial deployments.
  • Capability today:
  • High precision often requires large mass/ volume/ power instruments; miniaturised units typically suffer higher noise, reduced dynamic range and narrower coverage.
  • Capability needed:
  • Low-SWaP sensors with flagship-class sensitivity, dynamic range and calibration stability, enabled by advanced materials, microfabrication and on-chip processing.
  • Technologies:
  • TECH-1: Miniaturised Sensor Systems
  • TECH-2: Microelectronics
  • Space Sensing Applications:
  • SAPP-14: Distributed SmallSat Science Missions
  • Earth Applications:
  • EAPP-3: Precision Industrial Metrology
  • UN 2030 SDG Goals:
  • SDG-9: Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure
  • R&D Fields:
  • 2.2. Electrical engineering, electronic engineering, information engineering
  • 2.10. Nanotechnology
  • Sources:
  • SRC-3: Astrophysics Roadmap 2013 (Enduring Quests Daring Visions)

docs.title: AKRON-2: Extreme Environment Sensing Technology
docs.text:
• Taxonomy:
  • Sensors: Cross-cutting sensor subsystems
  • Gap summary:
  • Sensors and electronics that can operate reliably in extreme temperature, pressure, radiation, dust/corrosion regimes remain limited.
  • Capability today:
  • Partial solutions exist (rad-hard parts; limited high-temperature electronics) but often with short lifetimes, reduced precision, or heavy thermal protection.
  • Capability needed:
  • Long-duration, precision sensor chains and packaging for >500°C, high-pressure, high-radiation and corrosive/ dusty environments without bulky conditioning.
  • Technologies:
  • TECH-3: High-Temperature & Radiation-Hardened Sensors
  • TECH-4: Extreme Environment Electronics (SiC/ GaN/ diamond)
  • Space Sensing Applications:
  • SAPP-11: Planetary Science (Extreme Environment In-Situ Sensing)
  • SAPP-12: Space Environment Monitoring (Radiation & Particles)
  • Earth Applications:
  • EAPP-2: Radiation Safety and Nuclear Facility Monitoring
  • EAPP-6: Environmental Hazard Monitoring
  • UN 2030 SDG Goals:
  • SDG-9: Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure
  • SDG-13: Climate Action
  • R&D Fields:
  • 1.3. Physical sciences
  • 2.2. Electrical engineering, electronic engineering, information engineering
  • 2.5. Materials engineering
  • Sources:
  • SRC-3: Astrophysics Roadmap 2013 (Enduring Quests Daring Visions)

docs.title: AKRON-3: Far-Infrared Observation Capability
docs.text:
• Taxonomy:
  • Sensors: Photonic sensors
  • Gap summary:
  • Far-IR (terahertz) observation remains limited by detector sensitivity, cryogenic systems and suitable mission-class instruments, constraining space astronomy and Earth radiation budget science.
  • Capability today:
  • Few missions cover key far-IR bands; detector/readout performance depends on deep cryogenics; long-life cooling and large-format arrays remain hard to field.
  • Capability needed:
  • End-to-end far-IR systems (detectors + readout + long-life low-vibration cooling + calibration) enabling high spectral sensitivity and stable long-duration measurements.
  • Technologies:
  • TECH-5: Far-IR Detectors and Cryogenic Optics
  • TECH-6: Thermal Infrared Spectrometry
  • Space Sensing Applications:
  • SAPP-2: Astrophysics (Far-Infrared Astronomy)
  • Earth Applications:
  • EAPP-15: Climate Monitoring and Benchmarking
  • UN 2030 SDG Goals:
  • SDG-9: Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure
  • SDG-13: Climate Action
  • R&D Fields:
  • 1.3. Physical sciences
  • 2.2. Electrical engineering, electronic engineering, information engineering
  • Sources:
  • SRC-3: Astrophysics Roadmap 2013 (Enduring Quests Daring Visions)

docs.title: AKRON-4: Advanced UV/Optical Detector Technology
docs.text:
• Taxonomy:
  • Sensors: Photonic sensors
  • Gap summary:
  • UV/optical sensing is constrained by detector QE, noise, format size, and mirror/coating throughput and durability, limiting precision UV science and UV-based Earth/industrial measurements.
  • Capability today:
  • Existing UV detectors are relatively small, require intensive calibration, and degrade; coatings have limited reflectivity in far-UV and are contamination sensitive.
  • Capability needed:
  • Large-format low-noise high-QE UV/ optical arrays with durable high-reflectivity coatings and improved contamination tolerance for long-life operation.
  • Technologies:
  • TECH-7: UV-Visible Photon Detectors
  • TECH-8: Optical Coatings and Materials
  • Space Sensing Applications:
  • SAPP-1: Astrophysics (UV/Optical Astronomy)
  • SAPP-5: Heliophysics (Solar UV and X-ray Monitoring)
  • Earth Applications:
  • EAPP-1: Medical Diagnostic Imaging
  • EAPP-11: Public Health UV Exposure Monitoring
  • EAPP-14: Air Quality and Emissions Monitoring
  • UN 2030 SDG Goals:
  • SDG-3: Good Health and Well-being
  • SDG-9: Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure
  • SDG-13: Climate Action
  • R&D Fields:
  • 1.3. Physical sciences
  • 2.2. Electrical engineering, electronic engineering, information engineering
  • 2.5. Materials engineering
  • Sources:
  • SRC-4: On-Orbit Degradation of Solar Instruments

docs.title: AKRON-5: Quantum Sensor Technology for Fields and Gravity
docs.text:
• Taxonomy:
  • Sensors: Field & gravity sensors
  • Gap summary:
  • Quantum sensing promises major sensitivity gains for magnetic/gravity/inertial measurements but remains limited in deployable, robust, calibrated flight/field systems.
  • Capability today:
  • Lab and prototype demonstrations exist (quantum magnetometers, atom interferometers) but are bulky, power intensive, and sensitive to environmental perturbations.
  • Capability needed:
  • Compact robust quantum sensors with stable calibration, environmental tolerance, and validated performance for operational deployment in space and terrestrial settings.
  • Technologies:
  • TECH-9: Quantum Sensors (NV magnetometers, atom interferometers)
  • TECH-10: Precision Metrology
  • Space Sensing Applications:
  • SAPP-4: Astrophysics (Precision Spectroscopy & Photometry)
  • SAPP-6: Heliophysics (Magnetospheric & Plasma Field Mapping)
  • SAPP-10: Planetary Science (Gravity & Magnetic Field Surveys)
  • Earth Applications:
  • EAPP-3: Precision Industrial Metrology
  • EAPP-18: Infrastructure Subsurface Mapping
  • EAPP-19: Water Resource and Groundwater Management
  • UN 2030 SDG Goals:
  • SDG-6: Clean Water and Sanitation
  • SDG-9: Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure
  • R&D Fields:
  • 1.3. Physical sciences
  • 2.2. Electrical engineering, electronic engineering, information engineering
  • Sources:
  • SRC-5: Achieving Climate Change Absolute Accuracy in Orbit

docs.title: AKRON-6: Autonomous Intelligent Sensing Systems
docs.text:
• Taxonomy:
  • Autonomy: Onboard intelligence
  • Gap summary:
  • Remote sensing systems lack sufficient onboard intelligence to triage data and adapt observations in real time under comms constraints, reducing science/operational efficiency.
  • Capability today:
  • Most missions rely on ground-based planning; limited autonomy demos exist but are not widespread due to compute limits, validation burden and operational risk.
  • Capability needed:
  • Closed-loop autonomous sensing: onboard analysis, anomaly/event detection, adaptive retasking, and safe autonomy integrated with flight/field operations.
  • Technologies:
  • TECH-11: Onboard AI and Edge Computing
  • TECH-12: Autonomous Satellite Operations
  • TECH-13: Sensor Webs & Networks
  • Space Sensing Applications:
  • SAPP-15: Autonomous Deep-Space Observation
  • Earth Applications:
  • EAPP-5: Autonomous Industrial Operations
  • EAPP-9: Industrial Edge Analytics and IoT Sensing
  • EAPP-16: Disaster Risk Reduction and Emergency Response
  • UN 2030 SDG Goals:
  • SDG-9: Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure
  • SDG-11: Sustainable Cities and Communities
  • SDG-13: Climate Action
  • R&D Fields:
  • 1.2. Computer and information sciences
  • 2.2. Electrical engineering, electronic engineering, information engineering
  • Sources:
  • SRC-8: NASA Space Technology Roadmaps and Priorities (National Academies): Modelling, Simulation, and Information Technology

docs.title: AKRON-7: Space-Based Lidar for Atmospheric Sensing
docs.text:
• Taxonomy:
  • Sensors: Active sensing
  • Gap summary:
  • Spaceborne lidar for winds/trace gases/aerosols is constrained by long-life space-qualified lasers, receiver sensitivity and calibration, limiting active atmospheric profiling for Earth and other planets.
  • Capability today:
  • Limited heritage (e.g., single demonstrators); laser lifetime, stability and power constrain sustained operations; planetary atmospheric lidar largely absent.
  • Capability needed:
  • Operational long-life stable lidar transmit/ receive chains (Doppler, DIAL) with validated lifetime, stable calibration, and higher coverage/ precision.
  • Technologies:
  • TECH-14: Spaceborne Lidar (Doppler Wind Lidar, DIAL)
  • TECH-15: Laser Transmitters and Receivers
  • Space Sensing Applications:
  • SAPP-7: Heliophysics (Upper Atmosphere Dynamics)
  • SAPP-9: Planetary Science (Atmospheric Dynamics & Composition)
  • Earth Applications:
  • EAPP-15: Climate Monitoring and Benchmarking
  • EAPP-20: Weather Forecasting and Atmospheric Services
  • EAPP-14: Air Quality and Emissions Monitoring
  • EAPP-16: Disaster Risk Reduction and Emergency Response
  • UN 2030 SDG Goals:
  • SDG-9: Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure
  • SDG-11: Sustainable Cities and Communities
  • SDG-13: Climate Action
  • R&D Fields:
  • 1.5. Earth and related environmental sciences
  • 2.2. Electrical engineering, electronic engineering, information engineering
  • Sources:
  • SRC-11: Introducing Aeolus
  • SRC-12: Lidar-Measured Wind Profiles: The Need for Global Wind Observations

docs.title: AKRON-8: Subsurface Penetrating Radar & EM Sensing
docs.text:
• Taxonomy:
  • Sensors: Active sensing
  • Gap summary:
  • Subsurface characterisation is limited by radar/EM penetration vs resolution trade-offs and deployable architectures, constraining planetary subsurface science and terrestrial subsurface mapping.
  • Capability today:
  • Orbital/airborne radars provide limited depth or coarse resolution; EM methods are fragmented and not scalable for many targets; indirect inference remains common.
  • Capability needed:
  • Advanced low-frequency radar/ EM sounding with improved penetration and resolution, plus robust inversion/ validation for actionable subsurface products.
  • Technologies:
  • TECH-16: Ground-Penetrating Radar (Orbital/Aerial)
  • TECH-17: Electromagnetic Subsurface Sounding
  • TECH-18: Gravimetric Mapping Technologies
  • Space Sensing Applications:
  • SAPP-6: Heliophysics (Magnetospheric & Plasma Field Mapping)
  • SAPP-8: Planetary Science (Surface & Subsurface Characterisation)
  • SAPP-10: Planetary Science (Gravity & Magnetic Field Surveys)
  • Earth Applications:
  • EAPP-6: Environmental Hazard Monitoring
  • EAPP-18: Infrastructure Subsurface Mapping
  • EAPP-19: Water Resource and Groundwater Management
  • UN 2030 SDG Goals:
  • SDG-6: Clean Water and Sanitation
  • SDG-9: Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure
  • R&D Fields:
  • 1.3. Physical sciences
  • 1.5. Earth and related environmental sciences
  • 2.2. Electrical engineering, electronic engineering, information engineering
  • Sources:
  • SRC-3: Astrophysics Roadmap 2013 (Enduring Quests Daring Visions)

docs.title: AKRON-9: On-Orbit Calibration & Metrology Standards
docs.text:
• Taxonomy:
  • Calibration & Validation: Metrology
  • Gap summary:
  • High-accuracy sensing is constrained by insufficient SI-traceable calibration and long-term stability, limiting cross-mission comparability and decadal trend detection.
  • Capability today:
  • Pre-flight and vicarious calibration dominate; instrument drift and inter-sensor biases require empirical correction, limiting absolute accuracy for subtle signals.
  • Capability needed:
  • On-orbit SI-traceable reference and transfer methods (radiometric, spectral, geometric) plus ultra-stable instruments and cross-calibration infrastructure.
  • Technologies:
  • TECH-19: On-orbit Metrology and Calibration Systems
  • TECH-20: Ultra-Stable Instrumentation
  • Space Sensing Applications:
  • SAPP-4: Astrophysics (Precision Spectroscopy & Photometry)
  • SAPP-5: Heliophysics (Solar UV and X-ray Monitoring)
  • Earth Applications:
  • EAPP-3: Precision Industrial Metrology
  • EAPP-15: Climate Monitoring and Benchmarking
  • UN 2030 SDG Goals:
  • SDG-9: Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure
  • SDG-13: Climate Action
  • SDG-17: Partnerships for the Goals
  • R&D Fields:
  • 1.3. Physical sciences
  • 2.2. Electrical engineering, electronic engineering, information engineering
  • Sources:
  • SRC-5: Achieving Climate Change Absolute Accuracy in Orbit

docs.title: AKRON-10: Large-format low-noise X-ray focal plane arrays
docs.text:
• Taxonomy:
  • Sensors: High-energy sensors
  • Gap summary:
  • X-ray imagers lack large-area, fast, low-noise, radiation-tolerant focal plane arrays with stable calibration for next-generation performance targets.
  • Capability today:
  • Flight-qualified arrays require trade-offs between format, read noise, speed, power, and radiation tolerance; large formats remain limited in availability/maturity.
  • Capability needed:
  • Large-format low-noise high-speed radiation-tolerant arrays with stable gain/linearity and on-orbit calibration support for long-duration missions.
  • Technologies:
  • TECH-21: X-ray Imaging Detectors
  • Space Sensing Applications:
  • SAPP-3: Astrophysics (X-ray Astronomy)
  • SAPP-5: Heliophysics (Solar UV and X-ray Monitoring)
  • Earth Applications:
  • EAPP-1: Medical Diagnostic Imaging
  • UN 2030 SDG Goals:
  • SDG-3: Good Health and Well-being
  • SDG-9: Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure
  • R&D Fields:
  • 1.3. Physical sciences
  • 2.2. Electrical engineering, electronic engineering, information engineering
  • 2.5. Materials engineering
  • Sources:
  • SRC-1: High Definition X-ray Imager Technology Roadmap (Lynx HDXI)
  • SRC-2: The Silicon Meta-shell X-ray Mirror Technology Development Roadmap for the Lynx Mission

docs.title: AKRON-11: Lightweight high-throughput X-ray optics
docs.text:
• Taxonomy:
  • Sensors: High-energy sensors
  • Gap summary:
  • X-ray focusing optics remain mass/cost intensive; scaling effective area while maintaining high angular resolution is constrained by fabrication and metrology limits.
  • Capability today:
  • Heritage grazing-incidence optics provide good performance but are heavy and complex; next-gen missions require lighter, larger-area, higher-precision optics.
  • Capability needed:
  • Scalable lightweight X-ray optics with improved figure quality, alignment stability, and manufacturability for large-aperture observatories.
  • Technologies:
  • TECH-22: X-ray Focusing Optics
  • Space Sensing Applications:
  • SAPP-3: Astrophysics (X-ray Astronomy)
  • SAPP-4: Astrophysics (Precision Spectroscopy & Photometry)
  • Earth Applications:
  • EAPP-1: Medical Diagnostic Imaging
  • EAPP-17: Industrial Non-Destructive Testing (NDT)
  • UN 2030 SDG Goals:
  • SDG-3: Good Health and Well-being
  • SDG-9: Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure
  • R&D Fields:
  • 1.3. Physical sciences
  • 2.2. Electrical engineering, electronic engineering, information engineering
  • 2.5. Materials engineering
  • Sources:
  • SRC-1: High Definition X-ray Imager Technology Roadmap (Lynx HDXI)
  • SRC-2: The Silicon Meta-shell X-ray Mirror Technology Development Roadmap for the Lynx Mission

docs.title: AKRON-12: UV detector QE and longevity
docs.text:
• Taxonomy:
  • Sensors: Photonic sensors
  • Gap summary:
  • UV detectors are constrained by limited quantum efficiency, contamination sensitivity, and on-orbit degradation, impacting long-life UV measurements.
  • Capability today:
  • UV instruments require stringent contamination control and repeated calibration; QE and throughput often degrade under UV exposure and contamination.
  • Capability needed:
  • High-QE UV detector arrays with improved durability, contamination tolerance, and stable response over mission lifetimes.
  • Technologies:
  • TECH-2: Microelectronics
  • Space Sensing Applications:
  • SAPP-1: Astrophysics (UV/Optical Astronomy)
  • SAPP-4: Astrophysics (Precision Spectroscopy & Photometry)
  • SAPP-5: Heliophysics (Solar UV and X-ray Monitoring)
  • Earth Applications:
  • EAPP-1: Medical Diagnostic Imaging
  • EAPP-11: Public Health UV Exposure Monitoring
  • UN 2030 SDG Goals:
  • SDG-3: Good Health and Well-being
  • SDG-9: Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure
  • R&D Fields:
  • 1.3. Physical sciences
  • 2.2. Electrical engineering, electronic engineering, information engineering
  • 2.5. Materials engineering
  • Sources:
  • SRC-4: On-Orbit Degradation of Solar Instruments

docs.title: AKRON-13: Far-IR cryogenic detector/ readout maturity
docs.text:
• Taxonomy:
  • Sensors: Photonic sensors
  • Gap summary:
  • Far-IR science is constrained by deployable low-noise cryogenic detector+readout chains with long-life cooling, low microphonics and stable calibration.
  • Capability today:
  • Lab detectors approach fundamental limits, but fielding requires robust cryocoolers, low-noise readout, vibration control and calibration stability.
  • Capability needed:
  • Integrated far-IR instruments with large-format arrays, long-life low-vibration cooling, low-noise readout, and repeatable calibration in flight/ field.
  • Technologies:
  • TECH-23: Cryogenic Photon Detectors
  • TECH-24: Cryogenic Readout Electronics
  • TECH-25: Cryogenic Optics
  • Space Sensing Applications:
  • SAPP-2: Astrophysics (Far-Infrared Astronomy)
  • Earth Applications:
  • EAPP-3: Precision Industrial Metrology
  • UN 2030 SDG Goals:
  • SDG-9: Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure
  • SDG-13: Climate Action
  • R&D Fields:
  • 1.3. Physical sciences
  • 2.2. Electrical engineering, electronic engineering, information engineering
  • 2.3. Mechanical engineering
  • Sources:
  • SRC-3: Astrophysics Roadmap 2013 (Enduring Quests Daring Visions)

docs.title: AKRON-14: Deployable quantum magnetometers
docs.text:
• Taxonomy:
  • Sensors: Field & gravity sensors
  • Gap summary:
  • Quantum magnetometers are not broadly deployable as compact robust flight/field instruments with stable calibration and low SWaP.
  • Capability today:
  • High sensitivity demonstrated in lab; packaging, environmental control and calibration transfer remain barriers to operational deployment.
  • Capability needed:
  • Compact low-power quantum magnetometers with stable calibration, environmental tolerance and validated performance for operational missions and field use.
  • Technologies:
  • TECH-26: Quantum Magnetometers
  • Space Sensing Applications:
  • SAPP-5: Heliophysics (Solar UV and X-ray Monitoring)
  • SAPP-6: Heliophysics (Magnetospheric & Plasma Field Mapping)
  • SAPP-10: Planetary Science (Gravity & Magnetic Field Surveys)
  • Earth Applications:
  • EAPP-18: Infrastructure Subsurface Mapping
  • UN 2030 SDG Goals:
  • SDG-9: Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure
  • SDG-11: Sustainable Cities and Communities
  • R&D Fields:
  • 1.3. Physical sciences
  • 2.2. Electrical engineering, electronic engineering, information engineering
  • Sources:
  • SRC-3: Astrophysics Roadmap 2013 (Enduring Quests Daring Visions)

docs.title: AKRON-15: Ultra-stable pointing & thermal stability
docs.text:
• Taxonomy:
  • Platforms: Pointing, stability & disturbance control
  • Gap summary:
  • Precision sensing is constrained by platform micro-vibration, thermoelastic drift and pointing jitter that exceed detector/optics limits in long integrations.
  • Capability today:
  • Current spacecraft/platforms achieve good stability but struggle to meet ultra-stable regimes needed for photon-starved, high-dynamic-range measurements.
  • Capability needed:
  • System-level thermal-mechanical stability and disturbance isolation enabling sustained ultra-stable pointing and optical path stability over mission lifetimes.
  • Technologies:
  • TECH-27: Precision Attitude Control
  • TECH-28: Thermo-Mechanical Stability Engineering
  • TECH-29: Disturbance Isolation
  • Space Sensing Applications:
  • SAPP-4: Astrophysics (Precision Spectroscopy & Photometry)
  • Earth Applications:
  • EAPP-3: Precision Industrial Metrology
  • EAPP-7: Urban Environmental Monitoring
  • UN 2030 SDG Goals:
  • SDG-9: Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure
  • R&D Fields:
  • 2.2. Electrical engineering, electronic engineering, information engineering
  • 2.3. Mechanical engineering
  • Sources:
  • SRC-3: Astrophysics Roadmap 2013 (Enduring Quests Daring Visions)

docs.title: AKRON-16: Contamination control for optical throughput
docs.text:
• Taxonomy:
  • Platforms: Contamination & cleanliness
  • Gap summary:
  • Molecular/particulate contamination degrades optical throughput and calibration stability, limiting long-life precision sensing in both space and high-end terrestrial optics.
  • Capability today:
  • Contamination control is mission- and facility-specific; on-orbit and in-field throughput loss remains a recurring performance limiter.
  • Capability needed:
  • Predictive contamination models, material/process controls, and in-flight monitoring/mitigation to sustain optical performance and calibration integrity.
  • Technologies:
  • TECH-30: Spacecraft Contamination Control
  • TECH-31: Optical Surface Protection
  • Space Sensing Applications:
  • SAPP-1: Astrophysics (UV/Optical Astronomy)
  • SAPP-5: Heliophysics (Solar UV and X-ray Monitoring)
  • SAPP-8: Planetary Science (Surface & Subsurface Characterisation)
  • Earth Applications:
  • EAPP-3: Precision Industrial Metrology
  • UN 2030 SDG Goals:
  • SDG-9: Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure
  • R&D Fields:
  • 1.4. Chemical sciences
  • 2.5. Materials engineering
  • 2.11. Other engineering and technologies
  • Sources:
  • SRC-4: On-Orbit Degradation of Solar Instruments

docs.title: AKRON-17: Long-life low-vibration cryocoolers
docs.text:
• Taxonomy:
  • Platforms: Cryogenic systems
  • Gap summary:
  • Cryogenic sensing is constrained by long-life low-vibration cooling (2-10 K class) with high reliability and low microphonics.
  • Capability today:
  • Space cryocoolers exist but exported vibration and lifetime/reliability risk limit the most sensitive instruments and long campaigns.
  • Capability needed:
  • Qualified long-life low-vibration cryocoolers with validated reliability, reduced exported disturbance and lower power for precision instruments.
  • Technologies:
  • TECH-32: Space Cryocoolers (Stirling, pulse-tube, JT)
  • Space Sensing Applications:
  • SAPP-4: Astrophysics (Precision Spectroscopy & Photometry)
  • Earth Applications:
  • EAPP-1: Medical Diagnostic Imaging
  • EAPP-3: Precision Industrial Metrology
  • UN 2030 SDG Goals:
  • SDG-3: Good Health and Well-being
  • SDG-9: Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure
  • R&D Fields:
  • 2.2. Electrical engineering, electronic engineering, information engineering
  • 2.3. Mechanical engineering
  • Sources:
  • SRC-3: Astrophysics Roadmap 2013 (Enduring Quests Daring Visions)

docs.title: AKRON-18: High-performance rad-tolerant computing
docs.text:
• Taxonomy:
  • Electronics: Radiation-tolerant computing
  • Gap summary:
  • Onboard processing is constrained by the performance/efficiency gap between radiation-tolerant processors and modern compute needs for data-intensive sensing.
  • Capability today:
  • Rad-hard/rt processors lag commercial parts in throughput and MIPS/W, limiting onboard analytics, autonomy, and compression at the source.
  • Capability needed:
  • Radiation-tolerant multicore/ accelerated processors and toolchains with substantially higher performance per watt and validated reliability.
  • Technologies:
  • TECH-33: Radiation-Tolerant High-Performance Spaceflight Computing (HPSC-class)
  • Space Sensing Applications:
  • SAPP-2: Astrophysics (Far-Infrared Astronomy)
  • SAPP-12: Space Environment Monitoring (Radiation & Particles)
  • Earth Applications:
  • EAPP-2: Radiation Safety and Nuclear Facility Monitoring
  • EAPP-9: Industrial Edge Analytics and IoT Sensing
  • UN 2030 SDG Goals:
  • SDG-9: Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure
  • R&D Fields:
  • 1.2. Computer and information sciences
  • 2.2. Electrical engineering, electronic engineering, information engineering
  • Sources:
  • SRC-9: NASA’s High Performance Spaceflight Computing (HPSC): White Paper

docs.title: AKRON-19: Radiation-tolerant high-density memory
docs.text:
• Taxonomy:
  • Electronics: Radiation-tolerant memory
  • Gap summary:
  • High-capacity high-bandwidth memory with radiation tolerance and predictable error behaviour remains limiting for onboard sensing pipelines.
  • Capability today:
  • Space memory solutions are constrained in capacity/bandwidth; mitigation overhead and upset behaviour restrict buffering and in-situ analytics.
  • Capability needed:
  • High-density radiation-tolerant memory and controllers with characterised SEU/SEL behaviour, high bandwidth and low-overhead correction.
  • Technologies:
  • TECH-34: Radiation-Tolerant Space Memory Systems
  • Space Sensing Applications:
  • SAPP-12: Space Environment Monitoring (Radiation & Particles)
  • SAPP-15: Autonomous Deep-Space Observation
  • Earth Applications:
  • EAPP-3: Precision Industrial Metrology
  • UN 2030 SDG Goals:
  • SDG-9: Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure
  • R&D Fields:
  • 1.2. Computer and information sciences
  • 2.2. Electrical engineering, electronic engineering, information engineering
  • Sources:
  • SRC-3: Astrophysics Roadmap 2013 (Enduring Quests Daring Visions)

docs.title: AKRON-20: Ultra-low-noise front-end readout ASICs
docs.text:
• Taxonomy:
  • Electronics: Analogue & mixed-signal electronics
  • Gap summary:
  • Sensing performance is often limited by front-end electronics noise, dynamic range and power, particularly for large arrays and harsh environments.
  • Capability today:
  • ROIC/readout designs exist but trade noise vs speed vs power; scaling channels and achieving rad/extreme-environment robustness is difficult.
  • Capability needed:
  • Low-noise scalable ROIC/ASIC solutions with low power, radiation tolerance, stable linearity/gain and manufacturable integration for large arrays.
  • Technologies:
  • TECH-35: Readout Integrated Circuits (ROIC)
  • TECH-36: Low-Noise Analogue Electronics
  • Space Sensing Applications:
  • SAPP-1: Astrophysics (UV/Optical Astronomy)
  • SAPP-3: Astrophysics (X-ray Astronomy)
  • Earth Applications:
  • EAPP-1: Medical Diagnostic Imaging
  • EAPP-17: Industrial Non-Destructive Testing (NDT)
  • UN 2030 SDG Goals:
  • SDG-3: Good Health and Well-being
  • SDG-9: Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure
  • R&D Fields:
  • 1.3. Physical sciences
  • 2.2. Electrical engineering, electronic engineering, information engineering
  • Sources:
  • SRC-3: Astrophysics Roadmap 2013 (Enduring Quests Daring Visions)

docs.title: AKRON-21: Extreme-temperature electronics for sensing chains
docs.text:
• Taxonomy:
  • Electronics: Extreme-environment electronics
  • Gap summary:
  • End-to-end sensing chains lack validated operation across extreme temperatures/conditions for long durations while preserving precision (noise/ linearity/ drift).
  • Capability today:
  • Partial solutions exist but often need thermal conditioning and show reduced precision/lifetime under extreme temperature, pressure or radiation.
  • Capability needed:
  • Electronics, packaging and calibration strategies that maintain precision under extreme environments without heavy thermal management.
  • Technologies:
  • TECH-2: Microelectronics
  • Space Sensing Applications:
  • SAPP-5: Heliophysics (Solar UV and X-ray Monitoring)
  • SAPP-11: Planetary Science (Extreme Environment In-Situ Sensing)
  • Earth Applications:
  • EAPP-6: Environmental Hazard Monitoring
  • UN 2030 SDG Goals:
  • SDG-9: Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure
  • SDG-13: Climate Action
  • R&D Fields:
  • 1.3. Physical sciences
  • 2.2. Electrical engineering, electronic engineering, information engineering
  • 2.5. Materials engineering
  • Sources:
  • SRC-8: NASA Space Technology Roadmaps and Priorities (National Academies): Modelling, Simulation, and Information Technology

docs.title: Dictionaries
docs.text:
This folder contains the controlled vocabularies used across the Akron documentation: Technology Gap taxonomy, Technologies, Space Sensing Applications, Earth Applications, UN 2030 SDGs, OECD Frascati R&D Fields, and evidence Sources.

Together, these dictionaries provide consistent definitions and cross-references so list entries can be mapped and compared in a structured way.

docs.title: Earth Applications
docs.text: A structured inventory of applications dedicated solely to addressing problems and needs on Earth, using sensing, data, and analytics to support environmental management, infrastructure, industry, and society. This table includes only terrestrial use cases and explicitly excludes space exploration, space environment characterisation, or any applications whose primary purpose is beyond Earth.

docs.title: EAPP-1: Medical Diagnostic Imaging
docs.text: Advanced X-ray and related imaging systems for diagnosis, monitoring, and treatment planning in healthcare.

docs.title: EAPP-2: Radiation Safety and Nuclear Facility Monitoring
docs.text: Continuous monitoring of radiation levels in nuclear facilities and surrounding environments for safety and compliance.

docs.title: EAPP-3: Precision Industrial Metrology
docs.text: High-stability measurement systems ensuring calibration traceability and quality control in advanced manufacturing.

docs.title: EAPP-4: Critical Infrastructure Timing and Synchronisation
docs.text: High-precision timing for telecommunications, energy grids, financial transactions, and distributed industrial systems.

docs.title: EAPP-5: Autonomous Industrial Operations
docs.text: AI-enabled adaptive sensing and control in manufacturing, energy production, and logistics environments.

docs.title: EAPP-6: Environmental Hazard Monitoring
docs.text: Detection of volcanic activity, landslides, permafrost thaw, and other geophysical hazards affecting communities.

docs.title: EAPP-7: Urban Environmental Monitoring
docs.text: Dense sensor networks monitoring air quality, heat islands, and environmental stressors in cities.

docs.title: EAPP-8: Open Science and Long-Term Data Stewardship
docs.text: Preservation and interoperability of scientific datasets to enable reproducibility, transparency, and cross-disciplinary reuse.

docs.title: EAPP-9: Industrial Edge Analytics and IoT Sensing
docs.text: Local processing and intelligent analysis of high-volume sensor data within industrial and infrastructure systems.

docs.title: EAPP-10: Geodetic Surveying and Earth Deformation Monitoring
docs.text: Precision gravity, magnetic, and positioning measurements for tectonics, land subsidence, and Earth system science.

docs.title: EAPP-11: Public Health UV Exposure Monitoring
docs.text: Monitoring ultraviolet radiation exposure levels to inform public health advisories and prevention strategies.

docs.title: EAPP-12: Energy Sector Subsurface Exploration
docs.text: Electromagnetic and gravity-based sensing to characterise subsurface structures for geothermal and other energy resources.

docs.title: EAPP-13: Digital Preservation and Reproducible Analytics
docs.text: Long-term preservation of raw data and computational workflows enabling reliable reprocessing and regulatory traceability.

docs.title: EAPP-14: Air Quality and Emissions Monitoring
docs.text: Detection and quantification of atmospheric pollutants and greenhouse gases to support regulation, compliance, and public health protection.

docs.title: EAPP-15: Climate Monitoring and Benchmarking
docs.text: High-accuracy long-term monitoring of radiation balance, temperature trends, and atmospheric composition for climate attribution and policy support.

docs.title: EAPP-16: Disaster Risk Reduction and Emergency Response
docs.text: Rapid sensing and analytics to support response to floods, wildfires, storms, volcanic events, and industrial accidents.

docs.title: EAPP-17: Industrial Non-Destructive Testing (NDT)
docs.text: High-resolution inspection of materials and structures using X-ray and electromagnetic techniques without causing damage.

docs.title: EAPP-18: Infrastructure Subsurface Mapping
docs.text: Imaging underground utilities, foundations, and geological structures to reduce construction risk and asset failure.

docs.title: EAPP-19: Water Resource and Groundwater Management
docs.text: Mapping and monitoring subsurface water resources and hydrological change to support sustainable water planning.

docs.title: EAPP-20: Weather Forecasting and Atmospheric Services
docs.text: Operational wind, aerosol, and trace gas measurements supporting numerical weather prediction and severe weather warnings.

docs.text: Source: OECD (2015), Frascati Manual 2015: Guidelines for Collecting and Reporting Data on Research and Experimental Development, The Measurement of Scientific, Technological and Innovation Activities, OECD Publishing, Paris. DOI: http://dx.doi.org/10.1787/9789264239012-en

docs.title: Space Sensing Applications
docs.text: A structured inventory of applications that use space-based sensing exclusively for space exploration and space environment understanding. This table covers use cases focused on near-Earth space, cislunar space, and deep space, including planetary science, astrophysics, heliophysics, and space environment monitoring, explicitly excluding any applications intended to observe, analyse, or solve problems on Earth.

docs.title: SAPP-1: Astrophysics (UV/Optical Astronomy)
docs.text: Space-based observation of stars, galaxies, and cosmic phenomena in ultraviolet and optical wavelengths to study cosmic evolution and fundamental physics.

docs.title: SAPP-2: Astrophysics (Far-Infrared Astronomy)
docs.text: Space-based far-infrared observations of cold dust, star formation regions, and early-universe structures beyond atmospheric absorption limits.

docs.title: SAPP-3: Astrophysics (X-ray Astronomy)
docs.text: High-energy space-based observation of black holes, neutron stars, supernova remnants, and hot plasmas through X-ray imaging and spectroscopy.

docs.title: SAPP-4: Astrophysics (Precision Spectroscopy & Photometry)
docs.text: Ultra-stable space-based measurements of stellar brightness and spectra for exoplanet detection, cosmology, and fundamental physics experiments.

docs.title: SAPP-5: Heliophysics (Solar UV and X-ray Monitoring)
docs.text: Space-based sensing of solar radiation, flares, and high-energy emissions to characterise solar dynamics and space weather drivers.

docs.title: SAPP-6: Heliophysics (Magnetospheric & Plasma Field Mapping)
docs.text: In-situ and remote sensing of magnetic fields and charged particle populations in near-Earth and deep-space plasma environments.

docs.title: SAPP-7: Heliophysics (Upper Atmosphere Dynamics)
docs.text: Active and passive sensing of thermospheric and ionospheric winds and composition from space platforms.

docs.title: SAPP-8: Planetary Science (Surface & Subsurface Characterisation)
docs.text: Orbital and landed sensing of planetary surfaces and subsurfaces to detect ice, voids, geological layering, and structural features.

docs.title: SAPP-9: Planetary Science (Atmospheric Dynamics & Composition)
docs.text: Remote and in-situ sensing of planetary atmospheres to measure winds, trace gases, aerosols, and thermal structure.

docs.title: SAPP-10: Planetary Science (Gravity & Magnetic Field Surveys)
docs.text: Precision measurement of gravitational and magnetic fields of planetary bodies to infer internal structure and evolution.

docs.title: SAPP-11: Planetary Science (Extreme Environment In-Situ Sensing)
docs.text: Long-duration sensing on planetary surfaces under extreme temperature, pressure, and radiation conditions.

docs.title: SAPP-12: Space Environment Monitoring (Radiation & Particles)
docs.text: Continuous monitoring of energetic particles and radiation fields in near-Earth and deep-space environments.

docs.title: SAPP-13: Space Environment Monitoring (Dust & Plasma Interactions)
docs.text: Characterisation of dust populations and plasma interactions affecting spacecraft and planetary systems.

docs.title: SAPP-14: Distributed SmallSat Science Missions
docs.text: Constellations and small spacecraft conducting coordinated space-based sensing for planetary, heliophysical, or astrophysical objectives.

docs.title: SAPP-15: Autonomous Deep-Space Observation
docs.text: Self-directed sensing systems operating far from Earth with minimal communication latency and adaptive science targeting.

docs.title: Technologies
docs.text: A controlled catalogue of internationally recognised Technologies relevant to Space Sensing and Earth Applications. This table provides a common technical vocabulary for mapping Technology Gaps to specific solution domains, supporting consistency, interoperability, and cross-gap analysis.

docs.title: TECH-1: Miniaturised Sensor Systems
docs.text: Highly integrated low-SWaP sensing architectures maintaining high sensitivity and stability.

docs.title: TECH-2: Microelectronics
docs.text: Advanced microfabricated electronic components enabling compact, high-performance sensing systems.

docs.title: TECH-3: High-Temperature & Radiation-Hardened Sensors
docs.text: Sensors designed to operate reliably under extreme temperature and radiation conditions.

docs.title: TECH-4: Extreme Environment Electronics (SiC/ GaN/ diamond)
docs.text: Wide-bandgap semiconductor electronics for high-temperature and high-radiation environments.

docs.title: TECH-5: Far-IR Detectors and Cryogenic Optics
docs.text: Far-infrared sensing systems requiring cryogenic detectors and precision cold optics.

docs.title: TECH-6: Thermal Infrared Spectrometry
docs.text: Spectroscopic measurement of thermal infrared emission for composition and energy balance analysis.

docs.title: TECH-7: UV-Visible Photon Detectors
docs.text: Photon-counting or imaging detectors operating in ultraviolet and visible wavelengths.

docs.title: TECH-8: Optical Coatings and Materials
docs.text: Specialised reflective and transmissive coatings for high-throughput optical systems.

docs.title: TECH-9: Quantum Sensors (NV magnetometers, atom interferometers)
docs.text: Quantum-enabled devices for ultra-precise magnetic, gravity, and inertial measurements.

docs.title: TECH-10: Precision Metrology
docs.text: Technologies enabling ultra-accurate measurement, calibration, and traceability.

docs.title: TECH-11: Onboard AI and Edge Computing
docs.text: Embedded artificial intelligence for real-time data analysis and decision-making.

docs.title: TECH-12: Autonomous Satellite Operations
docs.text: Systems enabling spacecraft to self-manage sensing and operational tasks.

docs.title: TECH-13: Sensor Webs & Networks
docs.text: Distributed interconnected sensing architectures coordinating multiple platforms.

docs.title: TECH-14: Spaceborne Lidar (Doppler Wind Lidar, DIAL)
docs.text: Laser-based remote sensing systems measuring winds and atmospheric constituents.

docs.title: TECH-15: Laser Transmitters and Receivers
docs.text: High-stability laser emission and detection subsystems for active sensing.

docs.title: TECH-16: Ground-Penetrating Radar (Orbital/Aerial)
docs.text: Radar systems designed to image subsurface structures from above.

docs.title: TECH-17: Electromagnetic Subsurface Sounding
docs.text: EM techniques for characterising subsurface conductivity and structure.

docs.title: TECH-18: Gravimetric Mapping Technologies
docs.text: Technologies measuring gravitational variations to infer subsurface mass distribution.

docs.title: TECH-19: On-orbit Metrology and Calibration Systems
docs.text: Space-based reference systems providing SI-traceable calibration.

docs.title: TECH-20: Ultra-Stable Instrumentation
docs.text: Instruments engineered for minimal drift and exceptional long-term stability.

docs.title: TECH-21: X-ray Imaging Detectors
docs.text: High-energy photon detectors for X-ray imaging and spectroscopy.

docs.title: TECH-22: X-ray Focusing Optics
docs.text: Grazing-incidence optics concentrating X-rays for imaging applications.

docs.title: TECH-23: Cryogenic Photon Detectors
docs.text: Ultra-sensitive detectors operating at cryogenic temperatures.

docs.title: TECH-24: Cryogenic Readout Electronics
docs.text: Low-noise electronics designed for operation at cryogenic temperatures.

docs.title: TECH-25: Cryogenic Optics
docs.text: Optical assemblies engineered for stable performance at cryogenic temperatures.

docs.title: TECH-26: Quantum Magnetometers
docs.text: Quantum-based magnetic field sensors with extreme sensitivity.

docs.title: TECH-27: Precision Attitude Control
docs.text: High-accuracy spacecraft pointing and stability control systems.

docs.title: TECH-28: Thermo-Mechanical Stability Engineering
docs.text: Design approaches minimising thermal deformation and structural drift.

docs.title: TECH-29: Disturbance Isolation
docs.text: Mechanical systems reducing vibration and micro-disturbances.

docs.title: TECH-30: Spacecraft Contamination Control
docs.text: Methods preventing particulate and molecular contamination of instruments.

docs.title: TECH-31: Optical Surface Protection
docs.text: Coatings and treatments protecting optical elements from degradation.

docs.title: TECH-32: Space Cryocoolers (Stirling, pulse-tube, JT)
docs.text: Mechanical cryogenic coolers for long-duration space missions.

docs.title: TECH-33: Radiation-Tolerant High-Performance Spaceflight Computing (HPSC-class)
docs.text: Next-generation rad-tolerant processors for onboard data-intensive computation.

docs.title: TECH-34: Radiation-Tolerant Space Memory Systems
docs.text: High-reliability memory architectures resistant to radiation-induced faults.

docs.title: TECH-35: Readout Integrated Circuits (ROIC)
docs.text: Integrated circuits converting detector signals into digital outputs.

docs.title: TECH-36: Low-Noise Analogue Electronics
docs.text: Precision analogue front-end electronics minimising signal noise.

docs.title: TECH-37: Space-Qualified Atomic Clocks
docs.text: Atomic time standards engineered for space environments.

docs.title: TECH-38: Ultra-Stable Oscillators (USO)
docs.text: High-stability frequency references for precision timing applications.

docs.title: TECH-39: Time Transfer and Synchronisation Systems
docs.text: Technologies distributing precise time across instruments and platforms.

docs.title: TECH-40: Verification & Validation for Autonomous Systems
docs.text: Frameworks assuring safety and reliability of autonomous sensing systems.

docs.title: TECH-41: Digital Twins for Testing
docs.text: High-fidelity simulation environments for system validation.

docs.title: TECH-42: Optical Frequency Combs
docs.text: Laser sources providing precise evenly spaced spectral calibration lines.

docs.title: TECH-43: Metrology Test Infrastructure
docs.text: Facilities and reference systems for performance validation and calibration.

docs.title: TECH-44: Standardised Sensor Validation Protocols
docs.text: Repeatable benchmarking methods for sensor performance assessment.

docs.title: TECH-45: FAIR Data Stewardship
docs.text: Practices ensuring findable, accessible, interoperable, reusable data.

docs.title: TECH-46: Interoperable Metadata Standards
docs.text: Structured metadata frameworks enabling cross-system data exchange.

docs.title: TECH-47: Data Provenance Systems
docs.text: Technologies tracking data lineage and processing history.

docs.title: TECH-48: Integrity Verification Tooling
docs.text: Tools ensuring tamper-evident and trustworthy data pipelines.

docs.title: TECH-49: Measurement Uncertainty Propagation
docs.text: Frameworks propagating uncertainty through sensing and modelling chains.

docs.title: TECH-50: Probabilistic Inference Frameworks
docs.text: Statistical systems enabling uncertainty-aware decision-making.

docs.title: TECH-51: Long-Term Digital Preservation
docs.text: Strategies preserving digital data and context over decades.

docs.title: TECH-52: Reproducible Computational Workflows
docs.text: Executable pipelines ensuring consistent reprocessing and validation.

docs.title: TECH-53: Particle Detectors (solid-state, scintillator)
docs.text: Sensors detecting charged or neutral particles via solid-state or scintillation methods.

docs.title: TECH-54: Radiation-Hard Readout Electronics
docs.text: Detector readout electronics designed to withstand radiation environments.

docs.title: TECH-55: Advanced Sensor Packaging
docs.text: Miniaturised, low-outgassing packaging preserving calibration stability.

docs.title: TECH-56: Microelectronic Integration
docs.text: Techniques integrating multiple electronic functions into compact modules.

docs.title: TECH-57: Onboard Data Processing Pipelines
docs.text: Embedded processing architectures managing high-volume sensing data.

docs.title: TECH-58: Edge Analytics Infrastructure
docs.text: Distributed compute systems enabling real-time local data analysis.

docs.title: Technology Gap Taxonomy
docs.text: A reference table containing the highest-level functional domains of the space and Earth sensing ecosystem. Each record represents a mutually exclusive category of related Technology Gaps that share a common engineering constraint or capability domain.

docs.title: TAX-1: Autonomy
docs.text: Systems enabling autonomous decision-making, adaptation, and coordination.

docs.title: TAX-1.1: Autonomous planning & scheduling
docs.text: Closed-loop tasking, optimisation and retasking of sensing operations.

docs.title: TAX-1.2: Multi-agent coordination
docs.text: Cooperative sensing and coordination across multiple platforms/agents.

docs.title: TAX-1.3: Onboard data handling
docs.text: Edge processing, triage, compression and dataflow management onboard.

docs.title: TAX-1.4: Onboard intelligence
docs.text: AI/ML for perception, decision-making and adaptive sensing.

docs.title: TAX-1.5: Verification & validation
docs.text: Assurance, testing and certification methods for autonomous systems.

docs.title: TAX-2: Calibration & Validation
docs.text: Technologies ensuring measurement accuracy, traceability, and comparability.

docs.title: TAX-2.1: Cross-calibration & harmonisation
docs.text: Inter-instrument and inter-mission alignment and harmonisation methods.

docs.title: TAX-2.2: Drift & stability monitoring
docs.text: Long-term performance monitoring, drift detection and recalibration support.

docs.title: TAX-2.3: Metrology
docs.text: Measurement standards, traceability and calibration transfer methods.

docs.title: TAX-2.4: Validation infrastructure
docs.text: Testbeds, reference targets and benchmarking infrastructure.

docs.title: TAX-2.5: Wavelength & radiometric standards
docs.text: Absolute spectral/radiometric references and standards.

docs.title: TAX-3: Data Systems
docs.text: Data handling, integrity, interoperability, and long-term usability technologies.

docs.title: TAX-3.1: Data fusion & assimilation
docs.text: Frameworks for combining multi-source data and integrating models/assimilation.

docs.title: TAX-3.2: Data provenance & integrity
docs.text: Lineage, auditability, tamper evidence and trust mechanisms for data/products.

docs.title: TAX-3.3: Interoperability frameworks
docs.text: Standards, APIs and frameworks enabling reuse across systems and communities.

docs.title: TAX-3.4: Metadata & semantics
docs.text: Descriptive and machine-readable metadata, semantics and ontologies.

docs.title: TAX-3.5: Preservation & archiving
docs.text: Long-term storage, preservation and reproducible reprocessing support.

docs.title: TAX-3.6: Uncertainty & inference
docs.text: Uncertainty modelling/propagation and probabilistic inference for sensing products.

docs.title: TAX-4: Electronics
docs.text: Analogue, digital, and mixed-signal electronics enabling sensing, processing, and control.

docs.title: TAX-4.1: Analogue & mixed-signal electronics
docs.text: Front-end sensing electronics including amplification, filtering, conversion and readout.

docs.title: TAX-4.2: Extreme-environment electronics
docs.text: Electronics designed to operate under extreme temperatures, pressures and conditions.

docs.title: TAX-4.3: Packaging & integration
docs.text: Packaging, interconnects and integration approaches for reliable sensing electronics.

docs.title: TAX-4.4: Power electronics
docs.text: Power conversion, regulation, distribution and EMI/EMC control for sensing systems.

docs.title: TAX-4.5: Radiation-tolerant computing
docs.text: Processors and compute architectures designed to operate in radiation environments.

docs.title: TAX-4.6: Radiation-tolerant memory
docs.text: High-reliability storage and memory systems tolerant to radiation-induced faults.

docs.title: TAX-5: Platforms
docs.text: Structural, mechanical, thermal, and stability systems that host and enable sensors.

docs.title: TAX-5.1: Contamination & cleanliness
docs.text: Control of particulate and molecular contamination affecting sensor performance.

docs.title: TAX-5.2: Cryogenic systems
docs.text: Cooling systems enabling low-temperature instruments and detectors.

docs.title: TAX-5.3: Environmental hardening
docs.text: Resistance to radiation, dust, corrosion and other extreme operational environments.

docs.title: TAX-5.4: Mechanisms & deployables
docs.text: Moving/deployable mechanical subsystems, including scanners, gimbals and deployments.

docs.title: TAX-5.5: Pointing, stability & disturbance control
docs.text: Attitude control, jitter suppression, structural stability and disturbance isolation.

docs.title: TAX-5.6: Thermal control & thermoelastic stability
docs.text: Thermal management and thermoelastic effects that drive measurement stability and drift.

docs.title: TAX-6: Sensors
docs.text: Technologies that directly measure physical, chemical, or biological phenomena.

docs.title: TAX-6.1: Active sensing
docs.text: Transmit-receive sensing modalities such as lidar and radar used to probe atmosphere and subsurface structures.

docs.title: TAX-6.2: Cross-cutting sensor subsystems
docs.text: Shared sensor subsystems and enabling components that span multiple sensing modalities.

docs.title: TAX-6.3: Field & gravity sensors
docs.text: Sensors and methods for magnetic, gravity, and related field measurements.

docs.title: TAX-6.4: High-energy sensors
docs.text: Sensors and optics for high-energy regimes, including X-ray and other energetic photon observations.

docs.title: TAX-6.5: Photonic sensors
docs.text: Optical and photon-based sensing technologies, including UV, visible, infrared, and related readout chains.

docs.title: UN 2030 Sustainable Development Goals
docs.text: A reference table containing the official United Nations 2030 Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), used as a standardised framework for linking Earth Applications and Technology Gaps to globally recognised sustainability objectives.

docs.title: SDG-1: No Poverty
docs.text: • Goal: End poverty in all its forms everywhere.

docs.title: SDG-2: Zero Hunger
docs.text: • Goal: End hunger, achieve food security and improved nutrition, and promote sustainable agriculture.

docs.title: SDG-3: Good Health and Well-being
docs.text: • Goal: Ensure healthy lives and promote well-being for all at all ages.

docs.title: SDG-4: Quality Education
docs.text: • Goal: Ensure inclusive and equitable quality education and promote lifelong learning opportunities for all.

docs.title: SDG-5: Gender Equality
docs.text: • Goal: Achieve gender equality and empower all women and girls.

docs.title: SDG-6: Clean Water and Sanitation
docs.text: • Goal: Ensure availability and sustainable management of water and sanitation for all.

docs.title: SDG-7: Affordable and Clean Energy
docs.text: • Goal: Ensure access to affordable, reliable, sustainable and modern energy for all.

docs.title: SDG-8: Decent Work and Economic Growth
docs.text: • Goal: Promote sustained, inclusive and sustainable economic growth, full and productive employment and decent work for all.

docs.title: SDG-9: Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure
docs.text: • Goal: Build resilient infrastructure, promote inclusive and sustainable industrialization, and foster innovation.

docs.title: SDG-10: Reduced Inequalities
docs.text: • Goal: Reduce inequality within and among countries.

docs.title: SDG-11: Sustainable Cities and Communities
docs.text: • Goal: Make cities and human settlements inclusive, safe, resilient and sustainable.

docs.title: SDG-12: Responsible Consumption and Production
docs.text: • Goal: Ensure sustainable consumption and production patterns.

docs.title: SDG-13: Climate Action
docs.text: • Goal: Take urgent action to combat climate change and its impacts.

docs.title: SDG-14: Life Below Water
docs.text: • Goal: Conserve and sustainably use the oceans, seas and marine resources for sustainable development.

docs.title: SDG-15: Life on Land
docs.text: • Goal: Protect, restore and promote sustainable use of terrestrial ecosystems, sustainably manage forests, combat desertification, and halt and reverse land degradation and halt biodiversity loss.

docs.title: SDG-16: Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions
docs.text: • Goal: Promote peaceful and inclusive societies for sustainable development, provide access to justice for all and build effective, accountable and inclusive institutions at all levels.

docs.title: SDG-17: Partnerships for the Goals
docs.text: • Goal: Strengthen the means of implementation and revitalize the global partnership for sustainable development.
